title: Project Metadata, Python Packaging, Entry Points, Plugins, and Clean Installs · Program Brief: Console Entry
summary: The generator runs only from its repository, imports templates through the current directory, has unbounded dependencies, discovers arbitrary plugins, omits package data, and is never installed into a clean environment. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the console entry increment in installed reproducible static-site command. Learners progress t…
objectives: Expose an import-safe console script with argument parsing, status codes, stdout and stderr contracts, help, version, and module execution parity.
Declare build backend, project metadata, Python bounds, dependencies, optional groups, entry point, package data, license, and version source in pyproject.toml.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Console Entry · Theory Console Entry
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 5633 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to verify inspected package artifacts, clean command installs, resources, and plugin limits; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-packaging-cli-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-packaging-cli-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_packaging_cli_4(artifacts: int = 2, inspected: int = 2, clean_installs: int = 3, command_smokes: int = 3, unsafe_plugins: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if artifacts != 2 or inspected < 0:
        return False, inspected, "artifact-shape"
    if inspected != artifacts:
        return False, inspected, "artifact-inspection"
    if clean_installs < 2 or command_smokes != clean_installs:
        return False, command_smokes, "clean-install"
    if unsafe_plugins > 0:
        return False, unsafe_plugins, "plugin-boundary"
    assert inspected == artifacts
    return True, command_smokes, "installed-generator"

# Evidence: ssg-console-entry-19n7ci
# Competency: ssg-console-entry.
# Case 5324: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects.

def evidence_ssg_console_entry_19n7ci(artifacts: int = 2, inspected: int = 2, clean_installs: int = 3, command_smokes: int = 3, unsafe_plugins: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_19n7ci = "19n7ci-ic7n91-5324"
    assert evidence_variant_19n7ci
    evidence_axes_19n7ci = (
        "reader19n7ci",
        "source5324",
        "parseic7n91",
        "route19n7ci5324",
        "artifact532419n7ci",
        "recoveryic7n915324",
    )

    if inspected != artifacts:

    if clean_installs < 2 or command_smokes != clean_installs:

    if unsafe_plugins > 0:
        return False, unsafe_plugins, "plugin-boundary"
    assert inspected == artifacts
    return True, command_smokes, "installed-generator"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Console Entry model · Theory Console Entry
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Console Entry: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 3823 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to verify inspected package artifacts, clean command installs, resources, and plugin limits; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Expose an import-safe console script with argument parsing, status codes, stdout and stderr contracts, help, version, and module execution parity.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Console Entry model · Theory Console Entry.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Console Entry model · Theory Console Entry, keep Console Entry connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Expose an import-safe console script with argument parsing, status codes, stdout and stderr contracts, help, version, and module execution parity.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Console Entry model · Theory Console Entry, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Console Entry precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Console Entry model · Theory Console Entry, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 3823 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to verify inspected package artifacts, clean command installs, resources, and plugin limits; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Expose an import-safe console script with argument parsing, status codes, stdout and stderr contracts, help, version, and module execution parity.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Console Entry model · Theory Console Entry, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A console entry point receives command-line arguments automatically as parameters.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-console-entry-19n7ci.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-console-entry-19n7ci(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_console_entry_19n7ci\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?artifacts != 2 or inspected < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?inspected != artifacts)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?clean_installs < 2 or command_smokes != clean_installs)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unsafe_plugins > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
